semantics: The opaque agent handle maps to one live ZJS inspector session and events never cross sessions.
Every event is suppressed until HTTPServer.enable and again after HTTPServer.disable, detach, or release.
Request and response headers are parsed from JSON as even-length flat arrays containing only strings.
Optional request params are omitted for an empty BunString; otherwise they must be an even-length flat JSON string array.
HTTP method values preserve Bun Method.rs repr(u8) ordinals 0 through 35; every other value is rejected.
Request, response, body chunk, and handler error metadata are nested under the exact protocol object field.
Handler error url is omitted when empty rather than serialized as null or an empty placeholder.
A finite non-negative duration is emitted; NaN, negative, or negative-infinite duration means the optional field is absent.
Every required timestamp must be finite; invalid scalar, string, or JSON input emits no partial event.
Agent handles are registry-validated before dereference and retained across callbacks, including callback-driven release.
The Home and Bun profile libraries compile, link, and run one external consumer against all five exports.
